constraint_policy.definition: A constraint is an explicit, checkable requirement stated in the query or explicitly triggered by the provided context/source. Default expectations of financial professionalism, legality, or compliance are not constraints unless the sample explicitly states them.
exclude_examples: generic requirement to be compliant/legal/professional when not stated
unstated best practice inferred only from finance domain knowledge
external knowledge not present in the query or context

explicit_constraint_review: Merged items were checked for required fields, source_ids, and query/context-labeled constraints. Future QA should remove any constraint that cannot be traced to explicit query wording or explicit context/source text.
